message:
perf(mvcc): identity-hashed PageMap/PageSet in ConcurrentHandle hot paths

`ConcurrentHandle::{page_states, read_set, read_index, write_index}` use
std `HashMap`/`HashSet` keyed by `PageNumber`. Default std `RandomState`
is SipHash-1-3, which is ~70 cycles per probe for 32-bit-keyed lookups.
On UPDATE/DELETE hot paths these maps are probed once per page touched
in the transaction, and profile evidence showed the hashing at ~3-5%
of wall time.

`fsqlite_types::PageNumberBuildHasher` already exists and gives a
single-mov identity hash over the u32 key (PageNumbers are already
well-distributed sequential allocations, so weak hashing is fine here).

Swap the four HashMap/HashSet sites plus two view-borrow types in
`begin_concurrent.rs` to the aliased `PageMap`/`PageSet` types. Public
API unchanged: `read_set()` still returns a `&HashSet`-shaped type,
just with a different hasher. Behavior-preserving, zero-alloc delta.

Salvaged from abandoned ESO profiling worktree agent-a5f8c3dd (which
hit usage limit before benchmarking, so the claimed 3-5% improvement
is not yet independently measured — to be confirmed on next bench run).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
